100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 15, 1926, British Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin announced a significant reduction in coal production, aiming to stabilize the struggling industry amid ongoing labor disputes. In the United States, tensions rose as the Senate debated the future of Prohibition, with calls for reform gaining traction. Meanwhile, in Germany, the political climate remained volatile as the Weimar Republic faced increasing challenges from extremist factions.
Updated: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 07:50:50 AM CDT
